metadata.name: Repair a Damaged File System at Boot Time
metadata.description: Diagnose file-system issues and recover the system by fixing /etc/fstab errors
objectives: Diagnose file-system issues and recover the system
prerequisites: Familiarity with emergency and rescue boot targets

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server with file system issues to repair

tasks.name: Verify /etc/fstab has correct root mount point
tasks.description: The /etc/fstab file should have / as the mount point for the root filesystem, not /fakeroot

checks.description: Check that root filesystem is mounted on /

hints: The line should look like: UUID=<uuid> / xfs defaults 0 0
After editing, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' and 'mount -a' to verify

tasks.name: Verify root filesystem is mounted
tasks.description: The root filesystem should be mounted at /
tasks.failure_message: Root filesystem is not mounted correctly
hints: The root filesystem should be mounted as xfs

tasks.name: Verify /etc/fstab has valid syntax
tasks.description: The /etc/fstab file should be parseable without errors
hints: Each line should have: device mountpoint fstype options dump pass

tasks.name: Verify system can complete boot process
tasks.description: The system should be able to boot normally and reach multi-user target
tasks.failure_message: System did not complete boot process
hints: If the system is stuck, use emergency.target to troubleshoot
